title: The Federal Leash in {city.name}
trigger: The federal government threatens to cut all transit funding unless the city implements a wildly unpopular surveillance program.
description: The {city.government_body} is trapped between federal extortion and local riots. The {city.media_outlet} is already calling the mayor a federal puppet. Cameras are slated to go up on every corner near {city.landmark}.

options.label: Comply with the feds to keep the money flowing.
options.advisor_reason: We must accept the cameras to preserve our budget and social unity. The righteous have nothing to hide from the federal eye anyway.

consequences.follow_up: The cameras go up. Transit is funded but citizens start wearing masks everywhere to avoid facial recognition.
consequences.city_reaction: Big Brother is watching me wait for a delayed bus. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Refuse the cameras and sue the federal government.
options.advisor_reason: A 200M lawsuit is a steep price for our soul, but we cannot let federal overreach taint our moral standing. The people will praise our purity.

consequences.follow_up: The city loses the transit funding immediately. Buses stop running but the mayor is hailed as a rebel hero.
consequences.city_reaction: I am walking to work but at least I am free. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Install fake plastic cameras to trick the feds.
options.advisor_reason: Deception is a sin, but a 30M facade keeps the peace and the funding. Pray the federal ministers do not look too closely at our hollow virtue.

consequences.follow_up: The feds find out within a week. The city is fined heavily and the mayor is charged with fraud.
consequences.city_reaction: A plastic camera. Are we run by toddlers. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Knives Out in {city.name}
trigger: A coalition of angry council members tables a formal no-confidence motion against the administration.
description: They cite general incompetence and specific malice. Backroom deals are needed to survive the vote and the price of loyalty is skyrocketing. The {city.primary_industry_workers} are placing bets on the outcome.

options.label: Bribe undecided members with new infrastructure projects in their districts.
options.advisor_reason: Giving away 300M is a heavy penance, but it maintains the unity of our government. An open schism would be far too scandalous to bear.

consequences.follow_up: The administration survives the vote but the budget is completely hijacked by pork-barrel spending.
consequences.city_reaction: Surviving by emptying the treasury. Classic politics. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Publicly expose the corrupt secrets of the opposition leaders.
options.advisor_reason: We must purge their sins in the public square, even if the scandal risk tests our own faith. Righteousness requires a little dirt.

consequences.follow_up: The opposition collapses in scandal but the political atmosphere becomes incredibly toxic and vindictive.
consequences.city_reaction: They are all criminals, ours just had better dirt. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Lock the doors of the council chamber until they vote in your favor.
options.advisor_reason: Forced communion is still communion. A brief kidnapping is a small price for municipal harmony, though the police intervention severely tests our optics.

consequences.follow_up: It is technically kidnapping. The police have to breach the building. The mayor is briefly arrested.
consequences.city_reaction: Taking hostages to pass a budget. We are a banana republic. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Rat Leaves the Ship in {city.name}
trigger: The deputy mayor resigns on live television and joins the opposition party.
description: They take a box of highly sensitive documents with them. The {city.media_outlet} is having a field day with leaked internal emails which reveal the {city.government_body} has no idea what it is doing.

options.label: Launch a PR campaign claiming the defector was fired for incompetence.
options.advisor_reason: For 80M we can weave a narrative that protects our moral authority. It is a necessary falsehood to prevent the congregation from losing faith.

consequences.follow_up: Nobody really believes it but it muddies the waters enough to survive the news cycle.
consequences.city_reaction: Sure they were incompetent, but you hired them. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Sue the defector for violating their non-disclosure agreement.
options.advisor_reason: Dragging them through court costs 100M and invites wicked gossip, but apostates must be punished publicly to preserve our holy order.

consequences.follow_up: The lawsuit drags out all the dirty laundry in open court. It is a bloodbath for everyone involved.
consequences.city_reaction: Watching them destroy each other in court is my new soap opera. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Promote the defector's intern to deputy mayor to show you do not care.
options.advisor_reason: Elevating the meek saves us money and projects a serene, unified front. I am sure the intern's profound lack of experience will not spark any scandals.

consequences.follow_up: The intern accidentally deletes the city's entire zoning database on their first day. Absolute chaos ensues.
consequences.city_reaction: The intern is running the city. We are doomed. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Global Shame for {city.name}
trigger: An international human rights organization issues a scathing report on the treatment of {city.primary_industry_workers}.
description: Foreign investors are getting jittery. The {city.government_body} drafts a response calling the report foreign interference, but the {city.media_outlet} agrees with the report's findings.

options.label: Form a meaningless task force to study the report's findings.
options.advisor_reason: A 50M task force is the perfect liturgical response to international outrage. It looks deeply moral without actually disturbing our delicate status quo.

consequences.follow_up: The task force will meet once a year and do nothing. The international body moves on to another crisis.
consequences.city_reaction: A task force. The graveyard of accountability. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Implement sweeping labor reforms to appease the international community.
options.advisor_reason: Spending 250M on reforms cleanses our global reputation. The oligarchs will weep, but our optics on the world stage will be immaculately pure.

consequences.follow_up: The workers are thrilled but the local oligarchs threaten to pull all investments. The budget is strained.
consequences.city_reaction: Finally we are not an international embarrassment. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Ban the international organization from entering city limits.
options.advisor_reason: Excommunicating the UN avoids a protracted debate, though it invites terrifying global scandal. We must protect our flock from foreign judgment at all costs.

consequences.follow_up: Foreign investment drops to near zero. The city is treated like a rogue state by the international community.
consequences.city_reaction: We just sanctioned the UN. Bold strategy. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Trash War of {city.name}
trigger: The neighboring municipality starts illegally dumping raw garbage just across the border.
description: The smell is reaching {city.landmark}. Diplomatic talks have broken down into shouting matches over jurisdiction. The {city.government_body} is demanding action before the rats take over.

options.label: Pay to clean it up ourselves and build a barrier.
options.advisor_reason: Turning the other cheek costs 150M, but it cleanses the stench from {city.landmark}. Better a quiet, expensive peace than a scandalous neighborly dispute.

consequences.follow_up: We look weak but the smell goes away. The neighboring city laughs at us openly.
consequences.city_reaction: We are literally cleaning up their mess. Pathetic. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Load the trash into dump trucks and return it to their city hall lawn.
options.advisor_reason: Returning their filth is an ugly, vindictive act that risks a massive scandal spike. Yet the public demands righteous retribution, heaven help us.

consequences.follow_up: The citizens cheer the retaliation. The neighboring mayor declares a state of emergency.
consequences.city_reaction: Return to sender. Best day ever. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Annex the border territory using municipal police.
options.advisor_reason: An armed crusade over garbage is a moral catastrophe costing 200M. The resulting federal intervention will completely destroy our pious image.

consequences.follow_up: A minor border skirmish occurs. The federal government intervenes to stop the municipal war.
consequences.city_reaction: We are going to war over garbage. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Mutiny in {city.name}
trigger: A radical faction within the ruling party threatens to split and form their own coalition.
description: They demand extreme policy shifts and control over the budget committee. If they leave the government collapses. The {city.media_outlet} is running polls on who will backstab whom first.

options.label: Give them control of the budget committee to keep the peace.
options.advisor_reason: Sacrificing 100M and our moderate programs is a bitter pill, but preserving party unity is a moral imperative. Schisms only breed deeply embarrassing public shame.

consequences.follow_up: The government survives but the radicals immediately defund all moderate programs.
consequences.city_reaction: Surrendered the keys to the treasury to the crazy wing. Great. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Expel the faction leaders from the party entirely.
options.advisor_reason: Casting out the heretics purifies our ranks. The resulting political gridlock will be miserable, but at least our optics will be free of their radical taint.

consequences.follow_up: The government loses its majority and has to rule as a weak minority. Every vote is a bloodbath.
consequences.city_reaction: Finally grew a spine even if it means nothing gets done. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Resign from the party and govern as a fully independent executive.
options.advisor_reason: Abandoning our political church to walk alone invites absolute chaos and impeachment. The public disgrace will be a stain we can never wash away.

consequences.follow_up: The entire council unites against the mayor. Impeachment proceedings begin immediately.
consequences.city_reaction: Going full rogue. Let us see how this plays out. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: The Clock is Ticking in {city.name}
trigger: The opposition gathers enough signatures to force a recall election.
description: The {city.government_body} has 30 days to prove they are not completely useless or they will all be out of a job. The {city.media_outlet} is already running attack ads paid for by anonymous donors.

options.label: Launch a massive PR campaign highlighting minor successes.
options.advisor_reason: Emptying our coffers by 250M is a tragic waste, but we must broadcast a narrative of virtuous success to avoid the utter disgrace of a recall.

consequences.follow_up: The PR blitz works just enough to survive the recall by a razor-thin margin. The city is broke.
consequences.city_reaction: They spent our tax money to tell us they did a good job. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Challenge the opposition candidate to a live unscripted debate.
options.advisor_reason: An unscripted debate risks a terrifying loss of decorum. We must pray the opposition loses their temper first, keeping our own moral superiority intact.

consequences.follow_up: The debate is a screaming match. The mayor throws a glass of water. It is somehow a toss-up.
consequences.city_reaction: Jerry Springer style politics. I am voting for the water glass. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Invalidate the signatures on a technicality and cancel the election.
options.advisor_reason: Canceling democracy is a profound sin against public optics. The resulting riots will shatter the peaceful unity we have worked so hard to maintain.

consequences.follow_up: Riots erupt instantly. City hall is surrounded by angry mobs demanding democracy.
consequences.city_reaction: Canceling elections. We live in a dictatorship now. {city.slang_reaction}.

title: Tanks in the Streets of {city.name}
trigger: The local military garrison decides to help with traffic enforcement using armored personnel carriers.
description: Citizens are terrified. The general claims he is just restoring order but it looks a lot like a soft coup. The {city.government_body} is afraid to tell them to leave.

options.label: Politely request the federal government to recall the general.
options.advisor_reason: Waiting patiently for federal deliverance costs us 30M and looks weak, but it completely avoids the scandalous horror of armed conflict on our streets.

consequences.follow_up: The feds take weeks to respond. In the meantime tanks are issuing parking tickets near {city.landmark}.
consequences.city_reaction: Got pulled over by a tank today. Did not argue. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Order the municipal police to formally arrest the military personnel.
options.advisor_reason: Pitting our police against the army for 100M is a horrifying violation of civic peace. The public may cheer, but the resulting armed standoff is a scandal we cannot survive.

consequences.follow_up: A tense armed standoff occurs. The military eventually backs down but relations are permanently destroyed.
consequences.city_reaction: Cops vs Army. Buy popcorn. {city.slang_reaction}.

options.label: Appoint the general as the new head of the transport department.
options.advisor_reason: Embracing the usurper brings an unholy, terrifying order to our roads. It avoids a shootout, even if our moral authority is entirely surrendered to the sword.

consequences.follow_up: Traffic is solved through sheer terror. Jaywalkers are treated as enemy combatants.
consequences.city_reaction: No traffic jams when everyone is too scared to drive. {city.slang_reaction}.
